name: RDB and AOF Backup and Restore
description: Protect your Redis data with point-in-time RDB snapshots and append-only file (AOF) persistence for disaster recovery.
prerequisites: Write access to Redis data directory

steps.title: Trigger an RDB snapshot
steps.description: Force Redis to create a point-in-time dump file immediately.
steps.tip: BGSAVE forks a child process so the server stays responsive. Use LASTSAVE to check when it completed.

steps.title: Check last save timestamp
steps.description: Verify when the most recent RDB snapshot was completed.

steps.title: Locate the dump file
steps.description: Find the path to the RDB dump file on disk.
steps.command: redis-cli CONFIG GET dir && redis-cli CONFIG GET dbfilename

steps.title: Enable AOF persistence
steps.description: Turn on append-only file logging for near-realtime durability.
steps.warning: Enabling AOF on a running server triggers a rewrite. Ensure you have sufficient disk space.

steps.title: Copy backup files to safe storage
steps.description: Copy the RDB and AOF files to an external backup location.

steps.title: Restore from an RDB backup
steps.description: Stop Redis, replace the dump file, and restart to restore data.
steps.command: sudo systemctl stop redis && cp /backups/redis/dump-latest.rdb /var/lib/redis/dump.rdb && sudo systemctl start redis
steps.warning: Restoring replaces ALL current data. Make a backup of the current dump before restoring.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Redis Backup Script
set -euo pipefail

BACKUP_DIR="/backups/redis"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

echo "=== Triggering RDB snapshot ==="
redis-cli BGSAVE
sleep 2

echo ""
echo "=== Locating data files ==="
REDIS_DIR=$(redis-cli CONFIG GET dir | tail -1)
REDIS_DB=$(redis-cli CONFIG GET dbfilename | tail -1)

echo "Data directory: $REDIS_DIR"
echo "Dump file: $REDIS_DB"

echo ""
echo "=== Copying backup files ==="
if [ -f "$REDIS_DIR/$REDIS_DB" ]; then
  cp "$REDIS_DIR/$REDIS_DB" "$BACKUP_DIR/dump-$TIMESTAMP.rdb"
  echo "RDB backup: $BACKUP_DIR/dump-$TIMESTAMP.rdb"
fi

if [ -f "$REDIS_DIR/appendonly.aof" ]; then
  cp "$REDIS_DIR/appendonly.aof" "$BACKUP_DIR/appendonly-$TIMESTAMP.aof"
  echo "AOF backup: $BACKUP_DIR/appendonly-$TIMESTAMP.aof"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Backup complete ==="
ls -lh "$BACKUP_DIR"/*$TIMESTAMP* 2>/dev/null

faq.question: RDB vs AOF: which should I use?
faq.answer: Use both for best durability. RDB provides compact point-in-time snapshots for fast restores. AOF logs every write for minimal data loss (down to 1 second with appendfsync everysec).

faq.question: Does BGSAVE block the server?
faq.answer: No, BGSAVE forks a child process to write the dump file. The parent process continues serving requests. However, the fork itself can cause a brief latency spike on servers with large datasets.
